# ============================================================================
# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
# ============================================================================
#
# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
# Values can be overridden in:
#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#
# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
#
# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)

# ============================================================================
# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
# ============================================================================

# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
FEATURE_RESUME=true

# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Tracking: Request counting
FEATURE_TRACKING=true

# Reminder: Periodic static reminder injection (depends on tracking)
FEATURE_REMINDER=true

# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true

# ============================================================================
# LLM PROVIDER CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================================

# LLM provider selection
# Options: claude-cli | openai-api | groq | openrouter | custom
LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE CLI PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
# -haiku (4.5)  $1.00 / $5.00
# -sonnet (4.5) $3.00 / $15.00  (for in/out < 200k tokens)
# -opus (4.1)   $15.00 / $75
# ============================================================================

# Path to Claude CLI binary (auto-detect if empty)
# Searches: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
LLM_CLAUDE_BIN=

# Model name for Claude provider (used when LLM_PROVIDER=claude-cli)
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL=haiku

# ============================================================================
# OPENAI API PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
#
# -gpt-5-nano  $0.05 / $0.40
# -gpt-o4-mini $0.15 / $0.60
# -gpt-5-mini  $0.25 / $2.00
# ============================================================================

# OpenAI API key (required when LLM_PROVIDER=openai-api)
# Can also be set via OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY=

# OpenAI API endpoint
LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions

# Model name for OpenAI provider
# Options: gpt-o4-mini, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano
LLM_OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-5-nano

# ============================================================================
# GROQ API PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
#
# -openai/gpt-oss-20b            $0.075 / $0.30
# -meta-llama/llama-guard-4-12b  $0.20  / $0.20
# -llama-3.1-8b-instant          $0.05  / $0.08
# Models: openai/gpt-oss-20b, meta-llama/llama-guard-4-12b, llama-3.1-8b-instant
# ============================================================================

# GROQ API key (required when LLM_PROVIDER=groq)
# Can also be set via GROQ_API_KEY environment variable
LLM_GROQ_API_KEY=

# GROQ API endpoint
LLM_GROQ_ENDPOINT=https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions

# Model name for GROQ provider
# Options: openai/gpt-oss-20b, llama-3.1-8b-instant, mixtral-8x7b-32768
LLM_GROQ_MODEL=openai/gpt-oss-20b

# ============================================================================
# OPENROUTER API PROVIDER
#
# Pricing: Varies by model (see https://openrouter.ai/models)
# -google/gemma-3n-e4b-it          $0.02 / $0.04
# -openai/gpt-oss-20b              $0.03 / $0.14
# -meta-llama/llama-3-8b-instruct  $0.03 / $0.06
# -sao10k/l3-lunaris-8b            $0.04 / $0.05
# -google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite    $0.10 / $0.40
# ============================================================================

# OpenRouter API key (required when LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter)
# Can also be set via OPENROUTER_API_KEY environment variable
LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY=

# OpenRouter API endpoint
LLM_OPENROUTER_ENDPOINT=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions

#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION FEATURE
# ============================================================================
# LLM-based conversation analysis for goal tracking and clarity assessment
#
# Functions:
#   topic_extraction_get_clarity()    - Get clarity score from topic.json
#   topic_extraction_analyze()        - Run LLM analysis on transcript
#   topic_extraction_check_cadence()  - Check if cadence-based analysis is due
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_start()  - Launch sleeper polling process
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_loop()   - Sleeper polling loop (background)
#
# Dependencies: lib/common.sh, prompts/*.txt
# ============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# PRIVATE HELPERS
# ============================================================================

# Extract transcript excerpt for topic analysis
# Args: transcript_path
# Output: JSON array of preprocessed messages (configurable line count)
_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt() {
    local transcript_path="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        log_error "Transcript file not found: $transcript_path"
        echo "[]"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get configuration
    local line_count
    line_count=$(config_get "TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES")
    line_count=${line_count:-80}

    local filter_tools
    filter_tools=$(config_get "TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES")
    filter_tools=${filter_tools:-true}

    # Build jq filter for preprocessing
    local jq_filter='.message | select(. != null)'

    # Optionally filter out tool messages (tool_use and tool_result)
    if [ "$filter_tools" = "true" ]; then
        jq_filter+=' | select((.content | if type == "array" then (.[0].type != "tool_use" and .[0].type != "tool_result") else true end))'
    fi

    # Strip unnecessary attributes to reduce token usage
    jq_filter+=' | del(.model, .id, .type, .stop_reason, .stop_sequence, .usage)'

    log_debug "Extracting last $line_count lines with preprocessing (filter_tools=$filter_tools)"

    # Extract, preprocess, and wrap in array
    tail -n "$line_count" "$transcript_path" | jq -c "$jq_filter" | jq -s '.'
}

# Load prompt template and substitute transcript and previous topic
# Args: transcript_json, session_id
# Output: Prompt text with substituted transcript and previous topic
_topic_extraction_build_prompt() {
    local transcript_json="$1"
    local session_id="$2"

    local sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local prompt_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/topic-only.txt"

    if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
        log_error "Prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Read prompt template
    local prompt
    prompt=$(cat "$prompt_file")

    # Load previous topic.json if exists
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
    local previous_topic=""

    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        previous_topic=$(cat "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_debug "Loaded previous topic for comparison"
    else
        log_debug "No previous topic found (first analysis)"
    fi

    # Substitute placeholders
    # Use bash parameter expansion for safer substitution
    # This handles multi-line JSON and special characters
    prompt="${prompt//\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$previous_topic}"
    prompt="${prompt//\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_json}"

    echo "$prompt"
}

# Parse LLM output and write topic.json
# Args: session_id, llm_output
_topic_extraction_write_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local llm_output="$2"

    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    log_debug "Parsing LLM output for topic extraction"

    # Extract JSON from output (handles markdown wrapping)
    local json_output
    json_output=$(llm_extract_json "$llm_output")

    if ! json_validate "$json_output"; then
        log_error "Invalid JSON from LLM output"
        return 1
    fi

    # Select appropriate snarky comment based on clarity
    local clarity
    clarity=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.clarity_score // 5')

    local snarky_comment
    if [ "$clarity" -ge 7 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.high_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    else
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.low_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    fi

    # Rebuild JSON with single snarky_comment field
    json_output=$(echo "$json_output" | jq --arg snark "$snarky_comment" '. + {snarky_comment: $snark} | del(.high_clarity_snarky_comment, .low_clarity_snarky_comment)')

    # Backup existing topic.json before overwriting
    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        # Get timestamp in milliseconds (fallback to seconds if %N not supported)
        local timestamp_ms
        timestamp_ms=$(date +%s%3N 2>/dev/null || echo "$(date +%s)000")

        local backup_file="${session_dir}/${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
        mv "$topic_file" "$backup_file"
        log_debug "Backed up previous topic.json to: ${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    echo "$json_output" > "$topic_file"
    log_info "Topic file updated: $topic_file"

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PUBLIC API
# ============================================================================

# Get clarity score from topic.json
# Args: session_id
# Returns: Clarity score (1-10) or empty if not found
topic_extraction_get_clarity() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Get session directory (handles path resolution)
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id") || {
        log_debug "Cannot get session directory for: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    }

    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    if [ ! -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        log_debug "No topic file found for session: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate JSON first
    if ! json_validate "$(cat "$topic_file")"; then
        log_debug "Invalid JSON in topic file for session: $session_id"
        return 1
    fi

    local clarity
    clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    echo "$clarity"
}

# Run LLM-based analysis on transcript
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on failure
topic_extraction_analyze() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_info "Starting topic extraction analysis for session: $session_id"

    # Check if feature enabled
    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "topic_extraction"; then
        log_debug "Topic extraction feature disabled"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get model from provider-specific config
    local provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

    local model
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
            model=${model:-haiku}
            ;;
        openai-api)
            model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
            model=${model:-gpt-4-turbo}
            ;;
        custom)
            model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
            model=${model:-default}
            ;;
    esac

    log_debug "Topic analysis using model: $model"

    # Extract transcript excerpt
    local transcript_excerpt
    transcript_excerpt=$(_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt "$transcript_path")

    if [ -z "$transcript_excerpt" ] || [ "$transcript_excerpt" = "[]" ]; then
        log_warn "No transcript excerpt extracted, skipping analysis"
        return 0
    fi

    # Build prompt
    local prompt
    if ! prompt=$(_topic_extraction_build_prompt "$transcript_excerpt" "$session_id"); then
        log_error "Failed to build prompt"
        return 1
    fi

    # Invoke LLM
    log_debug "Invoking LLM for topic extraction"
    local llm_output
    if ! llm_output=$(llm_invoke "$model" "$prompt" 30); then
        log_error "LLM invocation failed"
        return 1
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    if ! _topic_extraction_write_topic "$session_id" "$llm_output"; then
        log_error "Failed to write topic file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if resume generation is needed
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    local significant_change=$(jq -r '.significant_change // false' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
    local clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    log_debug "significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity"

    if [ "$significant_change" = "true" ] && [ "$clarity" -ge 5 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Triggering resume generation (significant change detected, clarity=$clarity)"
        resume_generate_async "$session_id" "$transcript_path" || {
            log_warn "Failed to launch resume generation (non-fatal)"
        }
    else
        log_debug "Resume generation not needed (significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity)"
    fi

    log_info "Topic extraction analysis complete"
    return 0
}

# Check if cadence-based analysis is due
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir, response_count
# Returns: 0 if analysis should run, 1 if not
# Side effect: Launches background analysis if due
topic_extraction_check_cadence() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"
    local response_count="$4"

    log_debug "Checking cadence for response count: $response_count"

    # Get current clarity score
    local clarity
    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")

    # Default to low clarity if no topic file
    if [ -z "$clarity" ]; then
        clarity=5
    fi

    # Get cadence thresholds
    local clarity_threshold=$(config_get "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD")
    clarity_threshold=${clarity_threshold:-7}

    local high_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH")
    high_cadence=${high_cadence:-10}

    local low_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW")
    low_cadence=${low_cadence:-1}

    # Determine which cadence to use
    local cadence=$low_cadence
    if [ "$clarity" -ge "$clarity_threshold" ] 2>/dev/null; then
        cadence=$high_cadence
    fi

    log_debug "Clarity: $clarity, threshold: $clarity_threshold, cadence: $cadence"

    # Check if analysis is due
    if [ $((response_count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_info "Cadence-based analysis due at count $response_count (cadence: $cadence)"

        # Launch analysis in background directly
        local session_dir
        session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
        local pid_file="${session_dir}/analysis.pid"
        local log_file="${session_dir}/analysis.log"

        # Check if already running
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Analysis already running for session ${session_id}"
            return 0
        fi

        log_debug "Launching analysis process for session ${session_id}"

        local sidekick_root
        sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

        nohup bash -c "
            # Source common and feature libraries
            source '$sidekick_root/lib/common.sh'
            source '$sidekick_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'

            # Re-load config
            config_load

            # Re-initialize logging
            log_init '$session_id'

            # Run analysis
            topic_extraction_analyze '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
        " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

        local pid=$!
        echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
        log_debug "Launched analysis with PID ${pid}"

        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Analysis not due (count: $response_count, cadence: $cadence)"
    return 1
}

# Launch sleeper polling process in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success
topic_extraction_sleeper_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    # Check if sleeper enabled
    local sleeper_enabled=$(config_get "SLEEPER_ENABLED")
    sleeper_enabled=${sleeper_enabled:-true}

    if [ "$sleeper_enabled" != "true" ]; then
        log_debug "Sleeper disabled, skipping"
        return 0
    fi

    # Check if sleeper already running
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"

    if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Sleeper already running for session: $session_id"
            return 0
        else
            # Stale PID file
            log_debug "Removing stale sleeper PID file"
            rm -f "$pid_file"
        fi
    fi

    log_info "Launching sleeper process for session: $session_id"

    # Get paths
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    local script_path="${sidekick_root}/features/scripts/sleeper-loop.sh"

    # Verify script exists
    if [ ! -f "$script_path" ]; then
        log_error "Sleeper loop script not found: $script_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Launch script in background with environment variables
    nohup env \
        _SIDEKICK_ROOT="$sidekick_root" \
        CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" \
        bash "$script_path" "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir" "$sidekick_root" \
        > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
    log_debug "Launched sleeper with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

# Launch resume generation in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path
# Returns: 0 on success
resume_generate_async() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"

    log_info "Launching resume generation for session: $session_id"

    # Get paths
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    local script_path="${sidekick_root}/features/scripts/generate-resume.sh"

    # Verify script exists
    if [ ! -f "$script_path" ]; then
        log_error "Resume generation script not found: $script_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Launch script in background with environment variables
    nohup env \
        _SIDEKICK_ROOT="$sidekick_root" \
        CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" \
        bash "$script_path" "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$session_dir" "$sidekick_root" \
        > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    log_debug "Launched resume generation with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLUGIN HOOKS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit - UserPromptSubmit hook for topic extraction
#
# Ensures the sleeper process is running (watchdog pattern)
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_debug "Topic Extraction: UserPromptSubmit hook"

    # Ensure sleeper is running (watchdog pattern)
    # sleeper_start checks if already running and returns early if so
    if ! topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir"; then
        log_warn "Failed to ensure sleeper is running (non-fatal)"
    fi

    return 0
}

#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================================
# RESUME GENERATION SCRIPT
# ============================================================================
# Background process for generating session resume from topic analysis
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - session_dir
#   $4 - sidekick_root (for locating prompt templates)
#
# Environment:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - Sidekick installation directory
#   CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR - Project directory path
#
# This script is invoked as a background process by resume_generate_async()
# ============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# Arguments
session_id="$1"
transcript_path="$2"
session_dir="$3"
sidekick_root="$4"

# Source common library
script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
source "${script_dir}/../../lib/common.sh"

# Re-load config
config_load

# Re-initialize logging
log_init "$session_id"

log_info "Starting resume generation for session: $session_id"

# Read current topic.json
topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
if [ ! -f "$topic_file" ]; then
    log_error "Topic file not found for resume generation: $topic_file"
    exit 1
fi

current_topic=$(cat "$topic_file")

# Extract transcript excerpt (last ~3-5 messages, similar to topic-only)
if [ -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
    transcript_excerpt=$(tail -n 80 "$transcript_path" | jq -s '.' 2>/dev/null || echo '[]')
else
    log_warn "Transcript not found, using empty excerpt: $transcript_path"
    transcript_excerpt='[]'
fi

# Load prompt template
prompt_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/generate-resume.txt"
if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
    log_error "Resume prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
    exit 1
fi

# Build prompt with both topic and transcript
prompt=$(cat "$prompt_file")
prompt="${prompt//\{CURRENT_TOPIC\}/$current_topic}"
prompt="${prompt//\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_excerpt}"

# Get resume model from provider-specific config
provider=$(config_get 'LLM_PROVIDER')
provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

resume_model=""
case "$provider" in
    claude-cli)
        resume_model=$(config_get 'LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL')
        resume_model=${resume_model:-haiku}
        ;;
    openai-api)
        resume_model=$(config_get 'LLM_OPENAI_MODEL')
        resume_model=${resume_model:-gpt-4-turbo}
        ;;
    custom)
        resume_model=$(config_get 'LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL')
        resume_model=${resume_model:-default}
        ;;
esac

log_debug "Invoking LLM for resume generation (model: $resume_model)"

# Invoke LLM
if ! llm_output=$(llm_invoke "$resume_model" "$prompt" 30); then
    log_error "LLM invocation failed for resume generation"
    exit 1
fi

# Extract JSON from output
if ! resume_json=$(llm_extract_json "$llm_output"); then
    log_error "Failed to extract JSON from resume generation output"
    exit 1
fi

# Validate JSON
if ! echo "$resume_json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
    log_error "Invalid JSON from resume generation"
    exit 1
fi

# Write resume.json
resume_file="${session_dir}/resume.json"
echo "$resume_json" > "$resume_file"

log_info "Resume file created: $resume_file"

#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION FEATURE
# ============================================================================
# LLM-based conversation analysis for goal tracking and clarity assessment
#
# Functions:
#   topic_extraction_get_clarity()    - Get clarity score from topic.json
#   topic_extraction_analyze()        - Run LLM analysis on transcript
#   topic_extraction_check_cadence()  - Check if cadence-based analysis is due
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_start()  - Launch sleeper polling process
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_loop()   - Sleeper polling loop (background)
#
# Dependencies: lib/common.sh, prompts/*.txt
# ============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# PRIVATE HELPERS
# ============================================================================

# Extract transcript excerpt for topic analysis
# Args: transcript_path
# Output: JSON array of preprocessed messages (configurable line count)
_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt() {
    local transcript_path="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        log_error "Transcript file not found: $transcript_path"
        echo "[]"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get configuration
    local line_count
    line_count=$(config_get "TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES")
    line_count=${line_count:-80}

    local filter_tools
    filter_tools=$(config_get "TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES")
    filter_tools=${filter_tools:-true}

    # Build jq filter for preprocessing
    local jq_filter='.message | select(. != null)'

    # Optionally filter out tool messages (tool_use and tool_result)
    if [ "$filter_tools" = "true" ]; then
        jq_filter+=' | select((.content | if type == "array" then (.[0].type != "tool_use" and .[0].type != "tool_result") else true end))'
    fi

    # Strip unnecessary attributes to reduce token usage
    jq_filter+=' | del(.model, .id, .type, .stop_reason, .stop_sequence, .usage)'

    log_debug "Extracting last $line_count lines with preprocessing (filter_tools=$filter_tools)"

    # Extract, preprocess, and wrap in array
    tail -n "$line_count" "$transcript_path" | jq -c "$jq_filter" | jq -s '.'
}

# Load prompt template and substitute transcript and previous topic
# Args: transcript_json, session_id
# Output: Prompt text with substituted transcript and previous topic
_topic_extraction_build_prompt() {
    local transcript_json="$1"
    local session_id="$2"

    local sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local prompt_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/topic-only.txt"

    if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
        log_error "Prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Read prompt template
    local prompt
    prompt=$(cat "$prompt_file")

    # Load previous topic.json if exists
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
    local previous_topic=""

    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        previous_topic=$(cat "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_debug "Loaded previous topic for comparison"
    else
        log_debug "No previous topic found (first analysis)"
    fi

    # Substitute placeholders
    # Use bash parameter expansion for safer substitution
    # This handles multi-line JSON and special characters
    prompt="${prompt//\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$previous_topic}"
    prompt="${prompt//\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_json}"

    echo "$prompt"
}

# Parse LLM output and write topic.json
# Args: session_id, llm_output
_topic_extraction_write_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local llm_output="$2"

    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    log_debug "Parsing LLM output for topic extraction"

    # Extract JSON from output (handles markdown wrapping)
    local json_output
    json_output=$(llm_extract_json "$llm_output")

    if ! json_validate "$json_output"; then
        log_error "Invalid JSON from LLM output"
        return 1
    fi

    # Select appropriate snarky comment based on clarity
    local clarity
    clarity=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.clarity_score // 5')

    local snarky_comment
    if [ "$clarity" -ge 7 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.high_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    else
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.low_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    fi

    # Rebuild JSON with single snarky_comment field
    json_output=$(echo "$json_output" | jq --arg snark "$snarky_comment" '. + {snarky_comment: $snark} | del(.high_clarity_snarky_comment, .low_clarity_snarky_comment)')

    # Backup existing topic.json before overwriting
    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        # Get timestamp in milliseconds (fallback to seconds if %N not supported)
        local timestamp_ms
        timestamp_ms=$(date +%s%3N 2>/dev/null || echo "$(date +%s)000")

        local backup_file="${session_dir}/${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
        mv "$topic_file" "$backup_file"
        log_debug "Backed up previous topic.json to: ${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    echo "$json_output" > "$topic_file"
    log_info "Topic file updated: $topic_file"

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PUBLIC API
# ============================================================================

# Get clarity score from topic.json
# Args: session_id
# Returns: Clarity score (1-10) or empty if not found
topic_extraction_get_clarity() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Get session directory (handles path resolution)
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id") || {
        log_debug "Cannot get session directory for: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    }

    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    if [ ! -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        log_debug "No topic file found for session: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate JSON first
    if ! json_validate "$(cat "$topic_file")"; then
        log_debug "Invalid JSON in topic file for session: $session_id"
        return 1
    fi

    local clarity
    clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    echo "$clarity"
}

# Run LLM-based analysis on transcript
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on failure
topic_extraction_analyze() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_info "Starting topic extraction analysis for session: $session_id"

    # Check if feature enabled
    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "topic_extraction"; then
        log_debug "Topic extraction feature disabled"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get model from provider-specific config
    local provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

    local model
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
            model=${model:-haiku}
            ;;
        openai-api)
            model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
            model=${model:-gpt-4-turbo}
            ;;
        custom)
            model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
            model=${model:-default}
            ;;
    esac

    log_debug "Topic analysis using model: $model"

    # Extract transcript excerpt
    local transcript_excerpt
    transcript_excerpt=$(_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt "$transcript_path")

    if [ -z "$transcript_excerpt" ] || [ "$transcript_excerpt" = "[]" ]; then
        log_warn "No transcript excerpt extracted, skipping analysis"
        return 0
    fi

    # Build prompt
    local prompt
    if ! prompt=$(_topic_extraction_build_prompt "$transcript_excerpt" "$session_id"); then
        log_error "Failed to build prompt"
        return 1
    fi

    # Invoke LLM
    log_debug "Invoking LLM for topic extraction"
    local llm_output
    if ! llm_output=$(llm_invoke "$model" "$prompt" 30); then
        log_error "LLM invocation failed"
        return 1
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    if ! _topic_extraction_write_topic "$session_id" "$llm_output"; then
        log_error "Failed to write topic file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if resume generation is needed
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    local significant_change=$(jq -r '.significant_change // false' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
    local clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    log_debug "significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity"

    if [ "$significant_change" = "true" ] && [ "$clarity" -ge 5 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Triggering resume generation (significant change detected, clarity=$clarity)"
        resume_generate_async "$session_id" "$transcript_path" || {
            log_warn "Failed to launch resume generation (non-fatal)"
        }
    else
        log_debug "Resume generation not needed (significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity)"
    fi

    log_info "Topic extraction analysis complete"
    return 0
}

# Check if cadence-based analysis is due
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir, response_count
# Returns: 0 if analysis should run, 1 if not
# Side effect: Launches background analysis if due
topic_extraction_check_cadence() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"
    local response_count="$4"

    log_debug "Checking cadence for response count: $response_count"

    # Get current clarity score
    local clarity
    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")

    # Default to low clarity if no topic file
    if [ -z "$clarity" ]; then
        clarity=5
    fi

    # Get cadence thresholds
    local clarity_threshold=$(config_get "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD")
    clarity_threshold=${clarity_threshold:-7}

    local high_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH")
    high_cadence=${high_cadence:-10}

    local low_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW")
    low_cadence=${low_cadence:-1}

    # Determine which cadence to use
    local cadence=$low_cadence
    if [ "$clarity" -ge "$clarity_threshold" ] 2>/dev/null; then
        cadence=$high_cadence
    fi

    log_debug "Clarity: $clarity, threshold: $clarity_threshold, cadence: $cadence"

    # Check if analysis is due
    if [ $((response_count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_info "Cadence-based analysis due at count $response_count (cadence: $cadence)"

        # Launch analysis in background directly
        local session_dir
        session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
        local pid_file="${session_dir}/analysis.pid"
        local log_file="${session_dir}/analysis.log"

        # Check if already running
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Analysis already running for session ${session_id}"
            return 0
        fi

        log_debug "Launching analysis process for session ${session_id}"

        local sidekick_root
        sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

        nohup bash -c "
            # Source common and feature libraries
            source '$sidekick_root/lib/common.sh'
            source '$sidekick_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'

            # Re-load config
            config_load

            # Re-initialize logging
            log_init '$session_id'

            # Run analysis
            topic_extraction_analyze '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
        " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

        local pid=$!
        echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
        log_debug "Launched analysis with PID ${pid}"

        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Analysis not due (count: $response_count, cadence: $cadence)"
    return 1
}

# Launch sleeper polling process in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success
topic_extraction_sleeper_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    # Check if sleeper enabled
    local sleeper_enabled=$(config_get "SLEEPER_ENABLED")
    sleeper_enabled=${sleeper_enabled:-true}

    if [ "$sleeper_enabled" != "true" ]; then
        log_debug "Sleeper disabled, skipping"
        return 0
    fi

    # Check if sleeper already running
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"

    if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Sleeper already running for session: $session_id"
            return 0
        else
            # Stale PID file
            log_debug "Removing stale sleeper PID file"
            rm -f "$pid_file"
        fi
    fi

    log_info "Launching sleeper process for session: $session_id"

    # Get paths
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    local script_path="${sidekick_root}/features/scripts/sleeper-loop.sh"

    # Verify script exists
    if [ ! -f "$script_path" ]; then
        log_error "Sleeper loop script not found: $script_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Launch script in background with environment variables
    nohup env \
        _SIDEKICK_ROOT="$sidekick_root" \
        CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" \
        bash "$script_path" "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir" "$sidekick_root" \
        > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
    log_debug "Launched sleeper with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

# Launch resume generation in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path
# Returns: 0 on success
resume_generate_async() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"

    log_info "Launching resume generation for session: $session_id"

    # Get paths
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    local script_path="${sidekick_root}/features/scripts/generate-resume.sh"

    # Verify script exists
    if [ ! -f "$script_path" ]; then
        log_error "Resume generation script not found: $script_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Launch script in background with environment variables
    nohup env \
        _SIDEKICK_ROOT="$sidekick_root" \
        CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" \
        bash "$script_path" "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$session_dir" "$sidekick_root" \
        > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    log_debug "Launched resume generation with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLUGIN HOOKS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit - UserPromptSubmit hook for topic extraction
#
# Ensures the sleeper process is running (watchdog pattern)
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_debug "Topic Extraction: UserPromptSubmit hook"

    # Ensure sleeper is running (watchdog pattern)
    # sleeper_start checks if already running and returns early if so
    if ! topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir"; then
        log_warn "Failed to ensure sleeper is running (non-fatal)"
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Topic extraction feature loaded"

#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================================
# RESUME GENERATION SCRIPT
# ============================================================================
# Background process for generating session resume from topic analysis
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - session_dir
#   $4 - sidekick_root (for locating prompt templates)
#
# Environment:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - Sidekick installation directory
#   CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR - Project directory path
#
# This script is invoked as a background process by resume_generate_async()
# ============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# Arguments
session_id="$1"
transcript_path="$2"
session_dir="$3"
sidekick_root="$4"

# Source common library
script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
source "${script_dir}/../../lib/common.sh"

# Re-load config
config_load

# Re-initialize logging
log_init "$session_id"

log_info "Starting resume generation for session: $session_id"

# Read current topic.json
topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
if [ ! -f "$topic_file" ]; then
    log_error "Topic file not found for resume generation: $topic_file"
    exit 1
fi

current_topic=$(cat "$topic_file")

# Extract transcript excerpt (last ~3-5 messages, similar to topic-only)
if [ -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
    transcript_excerpt=$(tail -n 80 "$transcript_path" | jq -s '.' 2>/dev/null || echo '[]')
else
    log_warn "Transcript not found, using empty excerpt: $transcript_path"
    transcript_excerpt='[]'
fi

# Load prompt template
prompt_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/generate-resume.txt"
if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
    log_error "Resume prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
    exit 1
fi

# Build prompt with both topic and transcript
prompt=$(cat "$prompt_file")
prompt="${prompt//\{CURRENT_TOPIC\}/$current_topic}"
prompt="${prompt//\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_excerpt}"

# Get resume model from provider-specific config
provider=$(config_get 'LLM_PROVIDER')
provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

resume_model=""
case "$provider" in
    claude-cli)
        resume_model=$(config_get 'LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL')
        resume_model=${resume_model:-haiku}
        ;;
    openai-api)
        resume_model=$(config_get 'LLM_OPENAI_MODEL')
        resume_model=${resume_model:-gpt-4-turbo}
        ;;
    custom)
        resume_model=$(config_get 'LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL')
        resume_model=${resume_model:-default}
        ;;
esac

log_debug "Invoking LLM for resume generation (model: $resume_model)"

# Invoke LLM
if ! llm_output=$(llm_invoke "$resume_model" "$prompt" 30); then
    log_error "LLM invocation failed for resume generation"
    exit 1
fi

# Extract JSON from output
if ! resume_json=$(llm_extract_json "$llm_output"); then
    log_error "Failed to extract JSON from resume generation output"
    exit 1
fi

# Validate JSON
if ! echo "$resume_json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
    log_error "Invalid JSON from resume generation"
    exit 1
fi

# Write resume.json
resume_file="${session_dir}/resume.json"
echo "$resume_json" > "$resume_file"

log_info "Resume file created: $resume_file"

#     _LLM_COST_USD - "unknown" (OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response)
#     _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS - Input tokens used
#     _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS - Output tokens used
_llm_invoke_openrouter() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenRouter API key not found. Set LLM_OPENROUTER_API_KEY in config or OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenRouter API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ]
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    # Write payload to temp file to avoid ARG_MAX limits
    local payload_file
    payload_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$payload" > "$payload_file"

    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    local start_ms
    start_ms=$(date +%s%3N)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "@$payload_file" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    local end_ms
    end_ms=$(date +%s%3N)
    local wall_time_ms=$((end_ms - start_ms))

    # Cleanup payload file
    rm -f "$payload_file"

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "OpenRouter API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenRouter API returned error: ${error_msg}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract token usage from response
    _LLM_DURATION_MS="$wall_time_ms"
    _LLM_COST_USD="unknown"  # OpenRouter doesn't provide cost in response
    _LLM_INPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens // empty')
    _LLM_OUTPUT_TOKENS=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.usage.completion_tokens // empty')
